Taproom
Kitchen
933 Lee St SW, Atlanta, GA 30310
(opens in a new tab)
404.400.5068
The Space
The Garage is our barrel-aging and souring brewery in the heart of the historic West End of Atlanta. The taproom serves up small-batch and barrel-aged sours, craft cocktails and artisanal pizzas fired in a 900 degree Italian oven—made with a wild sourdough culture captured in our coolship. With 3 barrel rooms, an onsite orchard, and a spacious private events venue, The Garage is built for bold flavor and grand celebration.
The Taproom
Monday Night Garage offers expansive space along the Atlanta BeltLine, a popular community gathering space in the West End. With two indoor bars, The Garage offers more than 20 different beers on tap at all times, ranging from Monday Night Brewing favorites like Dr. Robot and Blind Pirate, to seasonal rotating beers, and special limited releases of impressive stouts, barrel aged sours, IPAs from the Hop Hut series and more. Crowlers and bottles of your new favorite beers are also available to-go.
Click Here for a Virtual Tour
The Brewery
Monday Night Brewing Garage is a fully-functioning production brewery that focuses on barrel-aged and sour beers. Our equipment includes:
4 stainless steel fermenters
2x 40 barrel foeders
A dedicated space for barrel-aged beers
Two dedicated areas for wild/sour beer aging
A coolship (affectionately known as the Crunkship)
A bottling line
An orchard to harvest local fruits, wild yeasts and bacteria
COCKTAILS
Monday Night Garage now pours a full lineup of craft cocktails, built around our house-made Sneaky Pete Spirits. Led by Brewmaster Peter Kiley — who brings his deep brewing and winemaking know-how to the art of distilling — this line includes our exclusive gin, vodka, bourbon, rye, and rum. From botanicals to barrel aging, each spirit reflects Peter’s signature craftsmanship (and a little mischief). Expect house-made syrups & infusions, seasonal specialties, martinis, spritzes, and yes—a great gin & tonic if that’s your style.
The Pizza
Our wood-fired pizzas are baked in an Italian-imported Neapolitan oven at over 900 degrees Fahrenheit. Made with only the freshest ingredients, we use grass-fed buffalo mozzarella imported from
Buf Creamery
, locally sourced greens, and fine meats. Fermented for three days in our
Crunkship
, our original dough is made with a homegrown yeast culture, Tipo 0 flour, and water.
Originally a venture born out of our co-Founder Joel Iverson’s passion for making pizza, you can read more about our pizza program in
Atlanta Magazine’s profile
.
Events
FAQs
Is your taproom dog friendy?
Well-behaved and leashed dogs are welcome on our taproom patios.
Do you allow people under 21?
Yes. We ID everyone. Underage people caught drinking will have to leave, along with their entire party.
We allow children at our brewery, and even have some toys for them. However, we ask that you keep a close eye on them, and please be mindful of the other patrons.
Can we buy beer to go?
Absolutely. Current Georgia laws allow us to sell 288 ounces of to-go beer per person, per day. That’s the equivalent of 24 12-ounce cans.
I’ve got a huge party coming up and want to buy some kegs from you. How do I do that?
We cannot legally sell kegs from the taproom. You can place a keg order from your local bottle shop.
Do you do growler fills?
We do not fill growlers at this time, but we do offer crowlers.
I don’t drink beer. What else do you have?
We offer a full cocktail menu, including zero-proof cocktails. We also offer soft drinks and kombucha.

--- Page: /about ---
About - Monday Night Brewing
About
Monday Night Brewing originally grew out of a small Atlanta Bible study. We started brewing beer together on Monday nights back in 2006 as a way to get to know each other better. As we learned more about brewing (and each other), we started making better, more complex beers. Friends, neighbors, and random strangers started joining us in our driveway to brew and commune together. Beer quickly became more than just a weeknight hobby.
Back in our homebrewing days, our three founders (
Jeff
,
Joel
, and
Jonathan
) had white collar day jobs. They wore neckties to work and would ceremoniously loosen their ties on Monday evenings before brewing that week’s batch of beer. The loosened necktie has remained with us as a symbol of doing what you love with your career, and making every day (not just weekends) exceptional.
We spent almost 5 years perfecting our beers before bringing them to market, launching in 2011 in Atlanta. Our
West Midtown
location opened up in early 2013, and is focused on core beer production and small batch hoppy beers. In September 2017, we opened our second facility. Monday Night
Garage
, located in southwest Atlanta, is our home for barrel-aged, sour beers and experimentation. Plus dinosaurs.
Meet The Staff
Careers at Monday Night
Giving Back

--- Page: /garage-private-events ---
Host Your Event At The Garage - Monday Night Brewing
Host Your Event At The Garage
The Garage is home to our barrel-aging and souring programs. Part of the Lee + White development along Atlanta’s BeltLine trail, the Garage is an eclectic, industrial-chic haven in the heart of the historic West End of Atlanta.
Check out our versatile options below. From weddings, to corporate events, to small-scale happy hours, we have unique spaces that will make your event delicious and memorable.
View Our Private Events Gallery

Chandelier Room
The Chandelier Room
features 5,000+ square feet of elegant, industrial-chic space inside our experimental small batch brewery. This space is ideal for large groups of 500+ people, and when combined with the Courtyard, can host up to 1000 people across a complete indoor/outdoor space.
Highlights include a full private bar, a private deck, vintage chandeliers spanning the ceiling, feature walls adorned with greenery and brewing elements, a catering prep kitchen, A/V suite, bridal suite, event staff and so much more.
The Chandelier Room provides a one-of-a-kind backdrop that makes your event completely unique and undeniably yours.
Ideal for:
Large gatherings, like weddings and fundraisers
Social gatherings, like graduations and receptions
Corporate conferences and meetings
Festivals, film shoots…and anything you can dream up!
Submit an Inquiry
Take a Virtual Tour
Courtyard
The Courtyard
is 4,500 sq ft. of lush, outdoor green space adjacent to our brewery. Spread out and settle in at biergarten tables surrounded by seasonally lit fire pits as you sip on a brew from your private craft beer bar.
The Courtyard is ideal for groups of 50-200, and can be rented as a full or partial venue. This area features a private entrance, sound system, and charming string lighting.
Ideal for:
Small social gatherings, like receptions, baby showers and engagement parties
Socially distanced outdoors gatherings, like happy hours and birthday parties
Pop-up marketing and small business showcases
Submit an Inquiry
Take a Virtual Tour
VIP Bar
The VIP Bar
is a semi-private, indoor space nestled within the Garage taproom, featuring views of our French Oak foeders and world-class brewing equipment. This area comes complete with a private bar (featuring up to 22 unique beers!) and  bartender.
The VIP Bar is ideal for parties of 50 or less seeking an exclusive experience that maintains all the energy and spark of the taproom. This space comes complete with velvet ropes…
